one_liner: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) lets developers manage Amazon Seller/Vendor Central data (orders, listings, inventory, reports) via OAuth-based (Login with Amazon) REST APIs, with self-serve registration for private apps and OAuth flow for public apps.

auth_methods.notes: Login with Amazon (LWA) OAuth2. Public apps use standard OAuth2 authorization code flow with sellers; private apps self-authorize via Solution Provider Portal. Refresh tokens (long-lived) exchanged for short-lived (1hr) access tokens via POST to https://api.amazon.com/auth/o2/token. Grantless operations use client_credentials grant. Restricted/PII operations require an additional Restricted Data Token (RDT) via the Tokens API, included via x-amz-access-token header.

self_serve_notes: Private applications (for a seller's own business data) remain free with self-serve registration and self-authorization via the Solution Provider Portal/Amazon Developer account. Public/third-party apps require Amazon developer registration and, starting Jan 31 2026, an annual $1,400 fee; per-call overage fees have been postponed. Docs, SDKs, and sandbox are free for all.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API surface covering catalog, orders, inventory, listings, pricing, reports, notifications, tokens, finances, and more across Seller Central and Vendor Central. SDKs available in multiple languages (Java, C#, etc.), plus sandbox testing environment.

main_blocker: None fundamental; requires Amazon developer account registration, app approval (for public apps), and OAuth/LWA credential management, plus new annual fee ($1,400) for third-party developers starting 2026. Multiple community MCP server implementations already exist.

evidence.supports: Describes LWA OAuth2 token exchange, refresh_token and client_credentials grant types, and RDT for restricted PII operations.
Details public app OAuth2 authorization workflow vs private app self-authorization via Solution Provider Portal.
Private applications can self-authorize directly, supporting self-serve-free path.
Third-party developers face new $1,400 annual fee starting Jan 31 2026; private apps remain free.
Confirms free tier limits and fee structure changes for 2026.
Example of an existing community-built MCP server for Amazon SP-API.

flags: Multiple unofficial/community MCP servers exist but no official Amazon-provided MCP server
New annual fee for third-party developers starting 2026 may affect cost model
Restricted/PII data requires additional RDT token flow, adding complexity
